commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: No automated repair is needed; maintainers need to accept or reject the compatibility/session-state tradeoff and then rely on normal CI for the exact PR head.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff changes local SQLite/plugin cache behavior and tests without new dependencies, workflows, scripts, or credential handling. (sha=6c8d05ca1a80950f88a58f0780576b3944264c12)
commands.review_summary:
This PR raises plugin-state and Telegram persistent message-cache row budgets to 3000, evicts oldest rows from the namespace being written before failing plugin-wide cap writes, and updates plugin-state and Telegram regression tests.

PR surface: Source +17, Tests +39. Total +56 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main counts all live plugin rows and throws once the plugin-wide 1000-row cap remains exceeded, which matches the reported Telegram message-cache plus topic-cache shape. I did not run a current-main failing probe because this review is read-only, but the PR body supplies an after-fix runtime probe on the real SQLite store.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is needed; maintainers need to accept or reject the compatibility/session-state tradeoff and then rely on normal CI for the exact PR head.

target.title: fix(plugin-state): evict current namespace on plugin row cap
target.body:
## Summary

- Make plugin-state shed old rows from the namespace that just grew before failing a plugin-wide row-limit write.
- Raise the plugin-state per-plugin row budget and Telegram persistent message-cache budget from 1000 to 3000 rows.
- Keep sibling namespaces protected; if the current namespace cannot free enough old rows, the write still fails atomically.
- Add regression coverage for the Telegram shape that failed in production, now sized against the 3000-row budget.

## Verification

- `PATH=/home/clawuser/.openclaw/tools/node-v22.22.0/bin:$PATH pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.sqlite.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.test.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.e2e.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts`
- `PATH=/home/clawuser/.openclaw/tools/node-v22.22.0/bin:$PATH OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.test.ts --reporter=dot --passWithNoTests=false` (`23 passed`)
- `PATH=/home/clawuser/.openclaw/tools/node-v22.22.0/bin:$PATH OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.e2e.test.ts -t "enforces the per-plugin live-row cap" --reporter=dot` (`1 passed | 11 skipped`)
- `PATH=/home/clawuser/.openclaw/tools/node-v22.22.0/bin:$PATH OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts --reporter=dot --passWithNoTests=false` (`16 passed`)
- `git diff --check`
- `PATH=/home/clawuser/.openclaw/tools/node-v22.22.0/bin:$PATH pnpm run tsgo:core`
- `PATH=/home/clawuser/.openclaw/tools/node-v22.22.0/bin:$PATH pnpm run tsgo:test:src`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram plugin state reached the old 1000 live-row plugin budget (message-cache plus topic-name-cache), so a new topic/message cache write threw `PluginStateStoreError` and surfaced as a generic Telegram processing failure.

Budget after fix: plugin-state allows 3000 live rows per plugin, and Telegram persistent message-cache allows 3000 entries.

Real runtime probe: On PR head `6c8d05ca1a`, I ran a Node 22.22.0 `tsx` probe against a temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` using the actual `createPluginStateKeyedStore` runtime API and the real plugin-state SQLite store. The probe seeded 2989 `telegram.message-cache` rows plus 11 `telegram.topic-name-cache` rows, then registered one new message-cache row.

Runtime output:

```text
PR_HEAD=6c8d05ca1a
STATE_DIR=/tmp/<temp>
DB_PATH=$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/plugin-state/state.sqlite
PLUGIN_CAP=3000
SEEDED_BEFORE message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000
REGISTER_NEW result=success key=msg-new
AFTER message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000
EVICTED_ORIGINAL_MESSAGE_KEYS=msg-0000
ASSERT new_message_present=true
ASSERT exactly_one_original_message_evicted=true
ASSERT topic_cache_preserved=true
ASSERT plugin_total_within_cap=true
DB_BYTES=585728
```

Evidence after fix: The real runtime probe successfully wrote the new Telegram message-cache row at the 3000-row plugin budget, evicted exactly one older message-cache row from the current namespace, preserved all 11 topic-name-cache rows, and kept the plugin total within cap.

Observed result after fix: Current-namespace cache growth no longer fails only because sibling plugin-state namespaces are also using rows, and the Telegram cache has a larger 3000-row runway.

What was not tested: A full live Telegram gateway rebuild on this PR head; production was manually unblocked by trimming old Telegram message-cache rows before this patch. The after-fix path was tested with the real plugin-state runtime API and SQLite store in an isolated runtime state directory.
target.author: keshavbotagent

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: keshavbotagent
commands.command: re-review please. submitted real runtime proof in the pr body for latest head `6c8d05ca1a`. this is not a vitest-only proof: i ran a node 22.22.0 `tsx` probe against a temporary `openclaw_state_dir` using the actual `createpluginstatekeyedstore` runtime api and real plugin-state sqlite store. runtime output: ```text pr_head=6c8d05ca1a state_dir=/tmp/<temp> db_path=$openclaw_state_dir/plugin-state/state.sqlite plugin_cap=3000 seeded_before message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000 register_new result=success key=msg-new after message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000 evicted_original_message_keys=msg-0000 assert new_message_present=true assert exactly_one_original_message_evicted=true assert topic_cache_preserved=true assert plugin_total_within_cap=true db_bytes=585728 ``` proof result: at the 3000-row telegram plugin budget, registering a new `telegram.message-cache` row succeeds, exactly one old message-cache row is evicted from the current namespace, all 11 sibling `telegram.topic-name-cache` rows remain present, and total live rows stay within cap.
commands.freeform_prompt: re-review please. Submitted real runtime proof in the PR body for latest head `6c8d05ca1a`. This is not a Vitest-only proof: I ran a Node 22.22.0 `tsx` probe against a temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` using the actual `createPluginStateKeyedStore` runtime API and real plugin-state SQLite store. Runtime output: ```text PR_HEAD=6c8d05ca1a STATE_DIR=/tmp/<temp> DB_PATH=$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/plugin-state/state.sqlite PLUGIN_CAP=3000 SEEDED_BEFORE message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000 REGISTER_NEW result=success key=msg-new AFTER message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000 EVICTED_ORIGINAL_MESSAGE_KEYS=msg-0000 ASSERT new_message_present=true ASSERT exactly_one_original_message_evicted=true ASSERT topic_cache_preserved=true ASSERT plugin_total_within_cap=true DB_BYTES=585728 ``` Proof result: at the 3000-row Telegram plugin budget, registering a new `telegram.message-cache` row succeeds, exactly one old message-cache row is evicted from the current namespace, all 11 sibling `telegram.topic-name-cache` rows remain present, and total live rows stay within cap.
